full_prompt: Design the primary airframe of an autonomous aerial robotics benchmark, inheriting a prior-mission payload and duration envelope. The vehicle must autonomously transport a 2 kg payload module to a ship mast 3 km offshore, deposit it with precision, and return home within a 9-minute flight window, all without GPS assistance once over water. Target configuration: coaxial octocopter, MTOW 12 kg, empty weight 8 kg, 4 kg battery (6S2P Li-ion 22000 mAh), 2 kg payload. Rotor diameter 0.66 m (26 inch) x 8 rotors; hover thrust per rotor 15 N, peak thrust 30 N. Airframe is carbon-fiber tube + printed PA12 joints: main tubes 25 mm OD x 22 mm ID CFRP pultruded (E = 140 GPa, Poisson 0.30, tensile 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3) length 500 mm cantilever from central hub, hub plate 2 mm CFRP laminate. Load cases: (LC1) hover at MTOW: 12 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 / 8 rotors = 14.7 N each; (LC2) peak evasive maneuver: 2 g lateral + 1.5 g vertical on full 12 kg; (LC3) 3 km out-and-back endurance: 9 minutes at 15 m/s cruise = 540 s of aerodynamic drag 10 N at body center; (LC4) wind gust: 15 m/s cross-gust encounter produces +/- 30% rotor thrust differential at one rotor = 9 N alternating; (LC5) payload release: sudden 2 kg mass release during precision drop = 19.6 N step-change at payload attachment point; (LC6) saltwater landing contingency (out-of-spec): 2 g on one arm during emergency water impact. Pass/fail: (R1) max ply strain in arms under LC1-LC4 <= 0.4%; (R2) hub plate strain under LC2 <= 0.3%; (R3) arm tip deflection under LC2 <= 15 mm; (R4) first arm bending mode above 40 Hz; (R5) total airframe mass (tubes + hub + motor mounts) <= 2.0 kg; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC2 compression phase >= 2.5. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, modal, buckling, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary airframe of an autonomous aerial robotics platform: a coaxial octocopter, MTOW 12 kg, that transports a 2 kg payload 3 km offshore to a ship mast, drops it with precision, and returns within a 9-minute window without GPS once over water. Arm material class: pultruded CFRP tubing; hub plate CFRP laminate. No external standard is named in the source; design to a 0.4% ply-strain allowable on the arms and 0.3% on the hub plate.

Envelope: 8 rotors of 0.66 m (26 in) diameter; arms 25 mm OD x 22 mm ID x 500 mm cantilever from the hub; hover thrust 15 N per rotor, peak 30 N; total airframe mass (tubes + hub + mounts) <= 2.0 kg. Battery 6S2P 22,000 mAh Li-ion.

Load cases.
- LC1 hover at MTOW: 14.7 N per arm (12 kg / 8).
- LC2 evasive: 2 g lateral + 1.5 g vertical on 12 kg.
- LC3 endurance drag: 10 N at body center for 540 s at 15 m/s cruise.
- LC4 gust: +/- 9 N alternating at one rotor (15 m/s cross-gust, 30% thrust differential).
- LC5 payload release: 19.6 N step-change at the payload attachment during drop.
- LC6 emergency water landing: 2 g on one arm.

prompt.title: Autonomous aerial robot airframe
prompt.brief: Coaxial octocopter, 12 kg MTOW, 3 km out-and-back autonomous GPS-denied flight.
